byRUDY AGUADO



EUREKA, Calif.  —On Thursday morning the third annual Law Enforcement Torch Run took place in Eureka.

Law Enforcement Personnel and Special Olympics athletes carried the Flame of Hope to the opening ceremony of local, regional, and even international competitions during the annual torch run for the Special Olympics.

"This means we get to see the faces and names of the people that are our law enforcement community. Special Olympics is fortunate to have been the charity of choice for the law enforcement community. And it is the number one supporter financially for Special Olympics," said Humboldt County Torch Run Coordinator, Mark Wheetley on what this means to the community.

As part of their efforts to raise awareness for the Special Olympics, law enforcement agencies raise funds and run the flame of hope through their communities.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
